---
title: "Intro to AI coding assistants"
subtitle: "Programming with LLMs for Data Practitioners"
author: "Julia Silge | Posit PBC"
footer: "<https://juliasilge.github.io/applied-stats-byu-2026/>"
highlight-style: atom-one
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, custom.scss]
    transition: fade
    preview-links: auto
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
    title-slide-attributes:
      data-background-image: "../images/undraw_coding-assistant_i178.svg"
      data-background-size: "auto 40%"
      data-background-position: "82% 85%"
      data-background-repeat: "no-repeat"
---

# Day 2: Build with AI {background-image="../images/undraw_coding-assistant_i178.svg" background-size="auto 40%" background-position="82% 85%" background-repeat="no-repeat"}
## What we'll cover today

1.  Intro to AI coding assistants
2.  Code completions and inline suggestions
3.  Chat-based assistance
4.  Agentic and autonomous workflows

## The landscape of options

TODO

## Modes: completion, chat, agent

TODO

## Cost considerations

TODO

## When to reach for which

TODO


# Thanks!

<center>

<img src="https://github.com/juliasilge.png" style="border-radius: 50%;" width="300px"/>

[{{< fa brands github >}} \@juliasilge](https://github.com/juliasilge)

[{{< fa brands bluesky >}} \@juliasilge.com](https://bsky.app/profile/juliasilge.com)

[{{< fa link >}} juliasilge.com](https://juliasilge.com/)

</center>

::: footer
Illustrations from [unDraw](https://undraw.co/) 
:::
